element_introduction_order.mastery_context: replaces dodge mechanic from previous games; enables spatial repositioning

element_introduction_order.element: Variable jump heights (small vs high jumps)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: faster approach for closing distance; shift from previous movement paradigm
resource gating for Super Special Moves

element_introduction_order.first_appears: early matches when Power Gauge fills
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: high-damage offensive option at full power gauge
maximum damage variant when low health + max power gauge; risk/reward decision point
tracking consecutive hits; first appearance in KOF series
Kyo vs Iori rivalry; emotional/narrative tension

element_introduction_order.element: Boss Team characters (Geese Howard, Wolfgang Krauser, Mr. Big)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: external IP crossover villains; skill check before final boss
Orochi servant; supernatural threat; narrative climax
tournament organizer; becomes playable option; bridges to sequel narrative

typical_relief_triggers: power gauge accumulation during favorable exchanges
typical_tension_triggers: character health bar flashing red (triggers critical super availability)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: tournament bracket structure with escalating opponent difficulty; rhythm built on match-by-match tension cycles within 3-character team depth; hand-to-hand emphasis creates shorter, more aggressive pressure cycles than projectile-heavy predecessors

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: unlockable Chizuru and Goenitz characters reward tournament completion/specific conditions

difficulty_curve.early_game: introductory team matches establish baseline mechanics and character familiarity; new evasion and running systems presented in low-pressure encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: rival team encounters (Iori narrative) increase technical demand; Kyo vs Iori confrontation serves as emotional/skill inflection point; introduction of advanced counter-technique timing
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss Team matches (Geese, Krauser, Mr. Big) represent significant skill gate with external IP weight; final Goenitz encounter escalates supernatural threat narrative and requires mastery of critical super mechanics
notable_spikes: transition from standard matches to Boss Team (external IP villains; known difficult characters from Fatal Fury/Art of Fighting series)
Goenitz final boss fight (supernatural servant of Orochi; requires knowledge of critical super conditions for optimal damage)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each match presents opportunity to rotate between three selected characters; team composition strategy creates meta-layer above individual character skill; encourages learning multiple matchups and character synergies
Mid-tournament matches feature story-significant opponent encounters (Iori Yagami) that carry narrative weight beyond mechanical challenge; creates emotional pacing beats within competitive structure

Boss Team composition pulls famous villains from other SNK properties (Geese Howard from Fatal Fury, Wolfgang Krauser, Mr. Big); serves as late-game skill verification and celebrates SNK's broader IP ecosystem
Goenitz encounter shifts from martial arts tournament to Orochi-sealing narrative; thematic escalation mirrors mechanical escalation (critical super mechanics required for optimal outcome)
